_description: Template for recording structured technical knowledge about PEM or Alkaline electrolysis technology. Copy this file, replace placeholder values, and save to /knowledge_base/technology_cards/{pem|alkaline}/.
_instructions: 2. Replace all <<PLACEHOLDER>> values with real data from authoritative sources
3. Technology cards should reference peer-reviewed literature, IEA/IRENA reports, or OEM datasheets
4. Each card represents a technology variant, not a specific product
5. Maintain version history — create a new card version when technology improves significantly

maturity.trl_rationale: <<Commercially deployed at >100 MW scale with multiple reference plants operating >2 years. Stack manufacturing at GW-scale capacity. Minor improvements ongoing in membrane durability and reduced iridium loading.>>
maturity.trl_assessed_by: <<IEA Hydrogen TCP / Internal assessment based on IEA 2025 report>>

infrastructure.power_supply_requirements: <<Requires stable DC power via transformer-rectifier. Compatible with intermittent renewable input (rapid ramp capability). Power quality: <5% THD recommended. Dedicated transformer typically required per 20 MW stack module.>>
infrastructure.water_infrastructure_needed: <<Ultra-pure water system required (ASTM Type II minimum, <1 µS/cm). Typical system includes: pre-treatment → RO → EDI → polishing loop. Closed-loop cooling water system for thermal management.>>
infrastructure.cooling_requirements: <<Approximately 15–20% of electrical input removed as heat. Cooling tower or dry cooler required. Can integrate with district heating if local offtake exists.>>
infrastructure.footprint_requirements: <<Approximately 50 m²/MW for electrolyzer hall. Additional space for BOP, substation, water treatment, storage. Total plant: ~200 m²/MW excluding renewable generation.>>
infrastructure.control_system_requirements: <<DCS with integrated safety instrumented system (SIS). Real-time monitoring of cell voltages, temperatures, pressures. Automated load-following capability for renewable integration.>>
infrastructure.workforce_skill_requirements: <<Operators require chemical/process engineering background. Specialized training on PEM stack handling, high-pressure hydrogen safety, and control systems. Typical staffing: 5–8 operators per shift for 100 MW plant.>>
infrastructure.maintenance_requirements: <<Preventive maintenance every 8,000 operating hours. Stack replacement at 60,000–80,000 hours (8–10 years). BOP maintenance aligned with standard chemical plant practices. Membrane inspection every 2 years.>>

applications.application_notes: <<Best suited where high-purity H₂ (>99.99%) is required without additional purification. Excellent for dynamic operation paired with intermittent renewables. Preferred for mobility applications due to high purity. Competitive in all major applications above 10 MW scale.>>

advantages: High hydrogen purity (>99.99%) without additional purification
Excellent dynamic response — suitable for intermittent renewable power
Wide operating range (5–100% load) without significant efficiency loss
Compact footprint compared to Alkaline at equivalent capacity
Pressurized hydrogen output (30 bar) reduces downstream compression needs
limitations: Dependence on iridium — one of the rarest elements on Earth
Higher stack cost than Alkaline due to precious metal catalysts and PFSA membranes
Stack lifetime (60,000–80,000 hours) shorter than Alkaline (80,000–100,000 hours)
PFSA membrane degradation accelerated by metal ion contamination
Titanium bipolar plates more expensive than nickel/stainless steel (Alkaline)
Limited number of large-scale OEMs (>20 MW modules) — supply chain concentration risk
Water quality requirements more stringent than Alkaline
technology_differentiators: Uses solid polymer electrolyte (PFSA membrane) instead of liquid KOH electrolyte
Proton (H⁺) conduction vs. hydroxide (OH⁻) conduction in Alkaline
Zero-gap cell design enables higher current density (2–3 A/cm² vs 0.2–0.5 A/cm²)
Fast-responding to load changes — better renewable integration profile
Pressurized operation simplifies downstream compression for most applications
improvement_trajectory: <<Near-term (2026–2030): Iridium loading reduction from ~0.5 to <0.1 g/kW through advanced catalyst structuring. Membrane thickness reduction for lower resistance. Stack size increase from 20 MW to 50 MW modules. Learning rate of ~15% expected to continue through 2030 as cumulative capacity doubles 3–4 times.>>

sources.title: <<Current status and future trends of PEM electrolysis for green hydrogen production — Energy & Environmental Science, 2024>>
<<IRENA Green Hydrogen Cost Reduction: Scaling up Electrolysers to Meet the 1.5°C Climate Goal, 2024 Update>>

rag_metadata.text_for_embedding: PEM Electrolysis — Pressurized — Large Scale. Type: PEM (Pressurized PEM, PFSA membrane). TRL 8 — early_commercial. Efficiency: 55 kWh/kg H₂. Stack lifetime: 60000 hours. Operating at 65°C. Typical plant scale: 10–500 MW. Advantages: High purity, dynamic response, compact footprint. Limitations: Iridium dependence, higher stack cost, shorter lifetime vs Alkaline. Critical materials: iridium, PFSA_membrane, titanium, platinum. Keywords: PEM, electrolysis, green hydrogen, pressurized, large scale, PFSA membrane, TRL8.
